content: The performance gap between intrinsic curiosity modules (ICM), random network distillation (RND), and bespoke curious agents can be closed by integrating 2024-2025 SOTA active learning memory architectures that prioritize preserving only high-outcome surprising episodic traces, rather than storing all encountered novel states. This creates a 'curiosity triage' framework where active learning memory pruning dynamically adapts the intrinsic reward signal to avoid both the distraction of uninformative novelty and the capacity collapse of overfull episodic memory stores.
source_topics: curiosity driven reinforcement learning ICM RND compared to curious agent
trigger_topic: curiosity driven reinforcement learning ICM RND compared to curious agent
